formalpara.title: Switch from CSLIBC to Newlib
formalpara.para: &csl_prod; now uses Newlib instead of CSLIBC. Refer to for more information about this change.

formalpara.title: Newlib update
formalpara.para: The Newlib package has been updated to version 2.2.0.

formalpara.title: Optimized strcmp
formalpara.para: strcmp has now been optimized for speed.

formalpara.title: Newlib update
formalpara.para: The Newlib package has been updated to version 2.1.0, with additions from the community CVS trunk as of 2014-06-03.

formalpara.title: New macros to access TCOpt
formalpara.para: The header file mips/mt.h now defines the macros mips32_gettcopt, mips32_settcopt and mips32_xchtcopt to access the CP0 register TCOpt.

formalpara.title: Optimized memset
formalpara.para: memset has now been optimized for speed.

formalpara.title: Newlib crash fix
formalpara.para: A bug that caused Newlib to sometimes crash when used with a C++ program that has more than 31 static objects has been fixed.

formalpara.title: Newlib update
formalpara.para: The Newlib package has been updated to version 2.0.0, with additions from the community CVS trunk as of 2013-07-06. This update provides various C99/POSIX cleanups along with other bug fixes and enhancements.

formalpara.title: ssize_t type definition bug fix
formalpara.para: The definition of the POSIX ssize_t data type has been corrected for 64-bit targets to match the width of the ISO C size_t type.

formalpara.title: E500 strcmp bug fix
formalpara.para: A bug has been fixed in the E500 version of the strcmp function. This caused unequal strings to compare as equal in some circumstances.

formalpara.title: sqrt bug fix
formalpara.para: A bug has been fixed in sqrt that caused frsqrte instructions to be used in multilibs for processors that do not support that instruction.

formalpara.title: Optimized square root functions
formalpara.para:
The performance of the functions sqrt and sqrtf has been improved for the following runtime libraries:
603 - 32-bit e600 (Altivec) - 32-bit
.

formalpara.title: Newlib update
formalpara.para: The Newlib package has been updated to version 1.18.0, with additions from the community CVS trunk as of 2010-08-12. This update provides additional wide-character functions, along with other bug fixes and enhancements.

formalpara.title: malloc fix
formalpara.para: A bug that sometimes caused free to dereference an invalid address has been fixed. The bug was caused by incorrect handling within malloc of calls to sbrk from outside of malloc.

formalpara.title: Optimized math functions for E500 cores
formalpara.para: Optimized implementations of log10, atan, scalbn, modf, log, ceil, abs, floor, and atan2 have been added for Freescale CPUs containing E500 cores, such as the 8540 and the 8548.

formalpara.title: Optimized library functions for E500 cores
formalpara.para: Optimized implementations of memcpy, memmove, memset, bzero, strlen, strcmp, and strcpy have been added for Freescale CPUs containing E500 cores, such as the 8540 and the 8548.

formalpara.title: Optimized memcpy
formalpara.para: The Newlib implementation of memcpy has been optimized to increase performance on ARM targets that support prefetch instructions.

formalpara.title: Support for controlling placement of library functions in memory
formalpara.para: Newlib is now built with -ffunction-sections to allow you to place a specific set of functions into an output section of your choice. For additional information, refer to .

formalpara.title: Optimized memcpy and memset routines
formalpara.para:
The Newlib implementations of memcpy and memset have been optimized to increase performance on ARM targets.
The NEON-enabled libraries available to Professional Edition subscribers also make use of vector instructions for additional performance improvements.

formalpara.title: Optimized string and memory functions
formalpara.para: The Newlib implementations of memcpy, memcmp, bzero, strcmp, strcpy, strlen and memset have been replaced with the versions of these functions that were formerly included with the SDE C library, and are optimized specifically for MIPS targets.

formalpara.title: Optimized math routines
formalpara.para: The Newlib implementations of rint, drem, sqrtf and sqrt have been replaced with the versions of these functions that were formerly included with the SDE math library, and are optimized specifically for MIPS targets.

formalpara.title: Newlib update
formalpara.para: The Newlib package has been updated to version 1.17.0, with additions from the community CVS trunk as of 2009-02-24. This update provides new C99 wide-character functions; POSIX regex functions; string-function performance improvements; an improved sprintf implementation that no longer requires I/O functions like _open, _write, and _close; and other bug fixes and improvements. For more information, refer to the Newlib C Library and Math Library manuals, and to the Newlib web site at http://sourceware.org/newlib/.

formalpara.title: Portable object file fixes
formalpara.para: Bugs in the limits.h, stdlib.h, time.h, and setjmp.h headers have been fixed. These bugs caused compilation errors when using -D_AEABI_PORTABILITY_LEVEL=1, as described in .

formalpara.title: Newlib
formalpara.para: The MIPS SDE Library has been replaced by the Newlib C library. This change should be transparent for applications not using SDE-specific features. Note that the related SDE toolkit, examples, and documentation have also been removed from this release.

formalpara.title: Object file portability
formalpara.para:
&csl_sgxx; for &csl_target_arch_name; EABI can now produce object files that are link-compatible with the GNU C Library included with &csl_sgxx; for &csl_target_arch_name; GNU/Linux.
These object files are additionally link-compatible with other ARM C Library ABI-compliant static linking environments and toolchains.
For additional information, refer to .

formalpara.title: Newlib manuals
formalpara.para: The documentation packaged with &csl_prod; now includes the Newlib C Library and Math Library manuals.

formalpara.title: C library reentrancy
formalpara.para: The C library is now reentrant.

formalpara.title: SIZE_MIN and SIZE_MAX
formalpara.para: SIZE_MAX is now correctly defined in stdint.h to be an unsigned quantity equal to the maximum possible value of a size_t, as required by the C standard. In addition, the definition of SIZE_MIN has been removed, as this constant is not prescribed by the C standard.

formalpara.title: ColdFire ROM script error
formalpara.para: A syntax error is fixed that prevented the ColdFire ROM scripts working for systems with ColdFire Flash Modules.

formalpara.title: VFP initialization code
formalpara.para: &csl_sgxx; now includes startup code to enable VFP coprocessors. This can be selected with -T rdimonvfp-ram.ld.

formalpara.title: Improved memcpy and memset
formalpara.para: memcpy and memset have been optimized for better performance.

formalpara.title: Execution order of .fini_array entries
formalpara.para: For ELF targets using .init_array and .fini_array sections, the functions whose addresses are stored in .fini_array are now run in reverse order (i.e., the last function in the array is run first), as required by the ELF specification. Previous releases ran the entries in forward order (i.e., the first function in the array was run first).

formalpara.title: Using internal SRAM
formalpara.para: The linker scripts now define additional memory regions such as .RAMBAR. Objects can be placed in these regions by using the __attribute__ ((section(".RAMBAR"))) attribute. Program start up initializes the contents of these regions.

formalpara.title: Linker scripts
formalpara.para: Board linker scripts are now only placed in the default multilib directory, or the multilib directory for the board in question. Therefore if you use an incorrect -mcpu option when compiling, you will get a link error rather than a program that will not run on your target board.

formalpara.title: Handling of out-of-range values by strtof
formalpara.para: The strtof function now sets errno to ERANGE when the input is not representable as a float, as required by the ISO C standard.

formalpara.title: New board support
formalpara.para: Support has been added for Cobra52235, Cobra5272, Cobra5282, Cobra5329, Cobra5475 and Cobra5485 boards.

formalpara.title: Semihosting compatibility improvements
formalpara.para: The semihosting I/O implementation has been improved to work with a wider range of simulators and host implementations.
